question: What does someone do with it?
title: What does someone do with it?
summary: Capture an idea, add notes, change its status, and return later to continue.

scenes.headline: One operator, one list of ideas, revisited over time.
scenes.body: The experience is deliberately ordinary: write something down, flesh it out, mark where it stands, and come back when you have more to say.

scenes.label: A typical session
scenes.description: Four steps someone takes when working with ideas.

steps.title: Capture an idea
steps.text: Give it a title and enough words to remember what you meant.

steps.title: Add notes and details
steps.text: Expand the record as the concept becomes clearer.

steps.title: Change its status
steps.text: Mark whether it is new, active, paused, or ready to build.

steps.title: Return later and continue
steps.text: Open the list again, pick an idea up, and keep developing it.

demo.label: Try a lightweight capture
demo.description: Illustrative only—this demo does not connect to a real database.
demo.placeholder: e.g. Shared invoice reminders for freelancers
demo.empty: No ideas yet. Capture one above.
